title: GBN-AG — GB Notification for Animals and Germinals (v1)
description: Defra import pre-notification schema for live animals and germinals. Derives from the canonical Defra UNVTD core (CertificatePayload)

allOf.description: Must contain a clause declaring the Reason for Import (identifier='PURPOSE'; urlId points at the ched_consignment_clause codelist).
Must contain a clause declaring the Animals Certified For category (identifier='GOODS_CERTIFIED_AS'; urlId points at the ched_consignment_clause codelist).

includedClause.description: When the PURPOSE clause content is INTERNAL_MARKET, an additional INTERNAL_MARKET_PURPOSE clause must be present declaring the fine-grained agricultural intent (slaughter / breeding / fattening / production / transfer of ownership). urlId points at the ched_consignment_clause codelist.

referenceDocument.description: Must carry at least one referenced document with typeCode='853' (UNTDID 1001 — Veterinary certificate). The accompanying-documents list (transport documents, ITAHC, etc.) shares this slot, distinguished by typeCode value.

importer.description: GBN-AG-specific re-inclusion: the party legally importing the consignment into GB customs territory. IPAFFS source: notification.partOne.importer. Not modelled by operational TRACES (TIG observes 'may be same party as consignee in SPS'); we keep them distinct because the UK regulatory pathway requires both.

finalDestinationLocation.description: GBN-AG-specific extension: the post-all-movements permanent destination, distinct from the immediate deliveryParty. The location identifier carries the County Parish Holding number with schemeId='cph_number'.

isOrHasUnweanedAnimals.description: CHED-A welfare indicator — true if the consignment contains any unweaned (non-ablacted) animals. Defra extension; no BSP analogue, no TRACES analogue. Follows BSP's isOrHas naming convention for consignment-level boolean indicators.

finalDestinationLocation.description: The permanent final destination location for the consignment - UK regulatory livestock tracking. identifier is the County Parish Holding (CPH) number as a bare string. The sibling urlId is pinned to the Defra-curated CPH register.

crossBorderRegulatoryProcedure.description: A cross-border regulatory procedure applied to the consignment - for GBN-AG, the SPS inspection at the UK Border Control Post. typeCode names the procedure; entryCustomsOfficeSpecifiedLogisticsLocation names the BCP. The BCP identifier carries its urlId naming the Defra-curated UK BCP register.

urlId.description: URL to the codelist this procedure typeCode is drawn from (e.g. regulatory_procedure_type).

gbnAgTradeLineItem.description: GBN-AG trade line item. Carries the trade product (species, classification, origin, per-animal data) and the line delivery (quantity). The additionalInformationNote slot carries Path A per-animal identifiers — the example populates BOTH placements so the joint review can compare with real data. No maxItems caps on the arrays — split-consignment / multi-product-line scenarios are not in scope today but adding caps would be premature optimisation.

additionalInformationNote.description: Path A per-animal identifiers (operational TRACES placement): each note carries subjectCode='INDIVIDUAL_IDENTIFICATION_NUMBER' with the identifier value in content. Populated alongside Path B (individualTradeProductInstance[]) for the joint review.

gbnAgTradeProduct.description: GBN-AG trade product: one species/commodity. CN classification, scientific and common names (resolved from the CN code via Defra reference data), country of origin with optional region, and per-animal identifiers Path B.

typeCode.description: Optional trade-product type/form code (live animal / semen / embryo / ova). BSP/D23B canonical (unece:typeCode, xsd:string). Drawn from a Defra-curated species-type codelist named by the sibling urlId.

urlId.description: URL to the codelist this trade product's typeCode is drawn from.

individualTradeProductInstance.description: Path B per-animal identifiers (BSP-canonical, purpose-built slot): each instance carries an identifier array (microchip / passport / tattoo / ear tag / leg ring as appropriate per the refdata identifier set for the species).

gbnAgProductClassification.description: Product classification. Required slots are systemId (the codelist name, e.g. 'CN' or 'SPECIES_CLASS') and classCode (the code value). No const enforcement on systemId — a single trade product can carry multiple classifications under different coding systems (e.g. CN code for commodity plus a SPECIES_CLASS entry for taxonomic class). CN codes are 8 digits in the operational case; the string-or-integer classCode accommodates both shapes without binding to a length pattern.

urlId.description: URL to the codelist this classification's classCode is drawn from.

originTradeCountry.description: Country of origin for the trade product, with optional region of origin (UNCL3227 functionTypeCode='106').

tradeCountrySubDivision.description: A sub-division of the country of origin (a region). functionTypeCode is constrained to '106' (UNCL3227 Region of Origin).

tradeProductInstance.description: An individual animal. identifier is an array of regulatory IDs (an animal can carry microchip + passport + tattoo + ear tag + leg ring together; the refdata identifier set for the species determines which apply). permanentLocation is the per-animal permanent address, where the animal will live long-term once import is complete; different animals on the same line can carry different permanent addresses. All properties are optional; the producer populates whichever apply.

items.description: A per-animal identifier. typeCode names the kind of identifier (EAR_TAG, PASSPORT, MICROCHIP, TATTOO, LEG_RING) and is a bare string drawn from the animal_identifier_types codelist. content is the identifier value itself - it is not drawn from a central registry (an ear-tag is issued by the farm; a microchip number is unique to the chip). The sibling urlId points to the codelist that typeCode is drawn from.

permanentLocation.description: Optional per-animal permanent address. The address where this individual animal will permanently reside after import (e.g. for pets imported to a business address and then shipped onwards to their owner's home). Shown for commodities where the producer's refdata indicates a permanent address applies.

gbnAgLineTradeDelivery.description: GBN-AG line delivery: the per-line quantity. The unit varies by commodity per Defra refdata routing — most live animals use H87 (piece count via numberOfAnimals); some commodities (e.g. Sus scrofa domesticus embryos/ova, commodity 203) use KGM (kilograms). The schema accepts any positive number with any string unit code; the source system enforces the refdata-driven unit per commodity.
